content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement
(916) 322-5662         322-5660           322-5901           322-6441


June 18, 1984


Donald A. MacLeod
Board of Directors
Costa Real Municipal Water Dist.
5950 El Camino Real
Carlsbad, CA 92008

                                    Re: Your Request for Advice, Our
                                        Advice No. A-84-089

Dear Mr. MacLeod:

     I am in receipt of your second letter requesting our advice
regarding the activities of Mr. Kubota and his contract for
engineering services to the district. You have expressed a
concern as to whether the contract is appropriate because you
are concerned that it may place Mr. Kubota in a conflict of
interest situation. You base your concern upon our advice
letter No. A-82-108. I am enclosing copies of two other advice
letters Nos. A-83-102 and A-82-029, along with a copy of
Commission Opinion No. 76-082 (Maloney Opinion, 3 FPPC Ops. 69)
for your review. By copy of this letter, I am asking for the
input of Mr. Kubota and the other members of the Board of the
District. When responses have been received so that we can be
sure that we have all the material facts from all parties
concerned, then we will render our advice.

                                    Sincerely,

                                    [signature]
                                    Robert E. Leidigh
                                    Counsel, Legal Division

REL:km

CC:  William C. Meadows, General Manager
     Paul S. Swirsky, Counsel to the District
     Members, Board of Directors

State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement
(916) 322-5662 322-5660 322-5901 322-6441

May 15, 1984


Donald A. MacLeod
755 Camellia Place
Carlsbad, CA 92008

Re: Your Request For Advice
Our Advice No. A-84-089

Dear Mr. MacLeod:

Please excuse the delay in my response to your letter. We
are unable to give you a confidential reply to your request. We
are also unable to provide you with advice regarding past
conduct by yourself or other persons. If you desire our advice
regarding your future duties and obligations, we would be
pleased to respond; albeit in a public letter.

Sincerely,

Robert E. Leigdh
Counsel, Legal Division

REL:km

sections.question_synthetic: Whether a contract between Costa Real Municipal Water District and Mr. Kubota for engineering services creates a conflict of interest situation under FPPC regulations.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC indicates it cannot provide confidential advice on past conduct and requires input from all parties, including Mr. Kubota and Board members, before rendering final advice on the matter.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains two related letters from May and June 1984. The initial May 15 letter declines to provide confidential advice on past conduct. The June 18 follow-up letter from FPPC requests input from Mr. Kubota and Board members before rendering advice. No formal CONCLUSION section exists; conclusion_synthetic is based on the substantive statements in both letters. References prior advice letters and Commission Opinion.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether a contract between Costa Real Municipal Water District and Mr. Kubota for engineering services creates a conflict of interest situation under FPPC regulations.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC indicates it cannot provide confidential advice on past conduct and requires input from all parties, including Mr. Kubota and Board members, before rendering final advice on the matter.

embedding.summary: This is an FPPC advice letter (A-84-089) regarding concerns about a contract between Costa Real Municipal Water District and Mr. Kubota for engineering services. The FPPC declines to give immediate confidential advice on past conduct but indicates it will provide public advice once all parties submit their responses with material facts.
